title: Machine Learning Workflow and Deep Learning Overview

learning_objectives: Understand the ML workflow from data to output.

blocks.title: Week 5 Focus
blocks.content: Students see why poor data leads to poor AI results.

items.label: Data Quality
items.description: Identify missing, inconsistent, or incorrect values.

items.label: ML Workflow
items.description: Understand collect, clean, train, test, and use.

items.label: Deep Learning
items.description: Recognize it as a layered learning method.

items.label: Clean
items.description: Fix errors, gaps, and formatting problems.

items.label: Train
items.description: Let the model learn from examples.

items.label: Evaluate
items.description: Check whether the result is useful and reliable.

blocks.title: Key Message
blocks.content: Poor input quality is one of the fastest ways to damage AI output quality.

activity.title: Data Quality Inspection
activity.instructions: Review a messy dataset and identify the biggest risks.
activity.success_criteria: Identify at least two major quality issues.

questions.prompt: Why does messy data create problems for AI systems?
options: Because it improves accuracy too much
Because poor input can lead to poor output
Because it always makes the app faster
questions.explanation: Poor-quality data often leads to poor-quality predictions or outputs.

questions.prompt: Which step comes early in a typical machine learning workflow?
questions.explanation: Collecting and cleaning data is an early foundation step.

questions.prompt: What is a useful high-level description of deep learning?
options: A method using layered neural networks
questions.explanation: Deep learning typically uses multiple layers to learn more complex patterns.

reflection.prompt: Name one data quality problem that could affect an AI result and explain why it matters.
reflection.placeholder: Missing values can make the system learn the wrong pattern or miss important context.

teacher_notes: Show a messy spreadsheet directly so students see the problem instead of only hearing about it.
Use the phrase 'garbage in, garbage out' carefully and explain it in plain language.
